segments.p1-8-g1: A line of direct speech marked by the dialogue dash; Ха repeated with hyphens is an onomatopoeic interjection representing laughter. There is no grammatical inflection here — it's simply a string of identical interjections used for audible effect.
segments.p1-8-g2: Colloquial opening: да here functions as a discourse particle (roughly 'but'/'well'), ведь adds emphatic 'after all', хотенья is the noun 'desires/wishes' and the enclitic -то attaches to it (хотенья-то) to mark contrast or emphasis. The phrase sets up the topic being commented on.
segments.p1-8-g3: в сущности is an idiomatic adverbial phrase meaning 'in essence' or 'essentially'; grammatically, в governs the noun сущности in its meaning here as a fixed expression rather than a literal locative.
segments.p1-8-g4: если хотите is a parenthetical conditional meaning 'if you like' or 'if you will'; если is the subordinating conjunction 'if', while хотите is 2nd person plural present indicative of хотеть used politely or generally ('you want/you please').
segments.p1-8-g5: и нет is an elliptical, emphatic negation: и here functions as an intensifier ('even' / 'and') and нет is the existential/definitive negation 'there is no' or 'do not exist'. The short exclamation makes the negation forceful.
segments.p1-8-g6: Typical spoken word order with the verb before the pronoun for emphasis: прерываете is 2nd person plural present indicative 'you interrupt', вы is the pronoun 'you' placed after the verb for stress, с + instrumental хохотом means 'with a burst/peal of laughter' (instrumental case used to indicate accompaniment/manner).
segments.p1-8-g7: Main clause: Наука is the feminine noun 'science' (subject). даже 'even' is emphatic. о сю пору до того is an archaic/colloquial temporal phrase ('up to this time' / 'to such an extent/degree'), with о сю (old form of 'эту/сию') + пору 'time/season' and до того intensifying 'to such a point'. успела is the perfective past (feminine) of успеть agreeing with наука and means 'has managed/succeeded', разанатомировать is a perfective infinitive 'to anatomize/dissect/analyze thoroughly', and человека is the direct object in the accusative 'man/person'.
segments.p1-8-g8: Subordinate connector + impersonal predicate: что is the conjunction 'that' introducing the result clause; уж is an emphatic particle ('already/indeed'); the sequence я теперь нам известно is an awkward, partly parenthetical ordering — the semantic core is нам известно ('it is known to us', an impersonal construction with dative 'нам' + short-form adjective/participle известно). The pronoun я appears as a parenthetical/inserted element emphasizing the speaker, but the grammatical predication is impersonal.
segments.p1-8-g9: Continuation of the result clause: что again introduces the content 'that', хотенье is nominative 'desire', и links the coordinated phrase 'and the so‑called free will' (так называемая is a passive participial adjective phrase meaning 'so‑called' or 'called thus'), свободная воля is the noun phrase 'free will'. есть is the copula 'is' (present), and не что иное is a fixed expression 'nothing other than' (не ... иное), with иное a neuter nominal meaning 'something else/other'.
segments.p1-8-g10: как is a comparative/connecting particle meaning 'as' or 'like'. Here it stands alone, leaving the comparison unfinished — a rhetorical trailing off. Grammatically it would normally introduce a simile clause, but the author leaves it unresolved.
segments.p1-8-g13: Постойте is the 2nd-person plural imperative (used as a polite or collective command) of постоять/подождать and here means "wait" or "hold on." The dash and comma show an interruption or address to the group.
segments.p1-8-g14: Господа is the plural form used vocatively to address several men—"gentlemen" or "sirs." It stands alone as the addressee after the imperative.
segments.p1-8-g15: Я is the nominative pronoun "I." И сам is an emphatic combination: и here means "also/even," and сам emphasizes the subject ("myself"/"I myself"). Так modifies начать: "to begin like that/that way." Начать is the perfective infinitive "to begin," and хотел is the past-tense masculine of хотеть; the sequence начать хотел (infinitive before past) is a common colloquial order equivalent to хотел начать — "(I) was going to / intended to begin."
segments.p1-8-g16: Я is the nominative pronoun "I," the sentence subject. The following comma marks a parenthetical comment.
segments.p1-8-g17: Признаюсь is the 1st-person singular present of признаться used parenthetically to mean "I must admit" / "I confess." Though the narration is largely past, this present-tense parenthetical is a rhetorical device to signal the speaker's admission.
segments.p1-8-g18: Даже is an adverb meaning "even," adding emphasis. Испугался is the perfective past (masculine) of испугаться, "to become frightened" or "to get scared," describing a completed reaction.
segments.p1-8-g19: Я только что is the time expression "I had just / just now." Хотел было is a common past construction where было after хотел softens it to mean "was about to" or an aborted/impending action; прокричать is the perfective infinitive "to shout out." Word order (infinitive before хотел) is colloquial but normal in speech.
segments.p1-8-g20: Что introduces the reported clause "that ..."; хотенье is a noun meaning "desire/wish." Ведь is a discourse particle "after all"/"you know." Черт знает (literally "the devil knows") is an idiomatic expression meaning "God/devil knows" or "who knows"; here знает is literal "knows." От чего зависит is the verb phrase "depends on what" (зависит governs the genitive/relative от чего). The following и что это begins the next substantiated clause "and that this..."
segments.p1-8-g21: Пожалуй is a parenthetical adverb meaning "perhaps" or "probably," used to soften or hedge the statement that follows.
segments.p1-8-g22: И is the coordinating conjunction "and" or a reinforcing particle here. Слава богу is an idiom literally "glory to God," used colloquially as "thank God" or "it's a good thing (thankfully)." The phrase expresses relief or that the outcome is fortunate.
segments.p1-8-g23: Да here is a colloquial particle meaning roughly "and then" / "well then" (it can also be an archaic 'and'); вспомнил is the past of вспомнить "I remembered"; про governs the accusative noun to mean "about/regarding;" науку is the accusative of наука "science/study," and the clitic -то on науку-то adds emphasis/pointing ("that very science"). The final и is a trailing conversational conjunction, softening or leaving the sentence feeling unfinished.
segments.p1-8-g26: Оселся is the past (masculine) reflexive form of осесть, which can mean "to sit down," "to settle (oneself)," or "to sink/settle." Here it describes the subject taking a seat or settling down; the reflexive -ся indicates the action is performed by/affects the subject.
segments.p1-8-g72: Ведь is a discourse particle ('after all'). Если introduces a conditional. Хотенье is the nominative subject 'desire' (archaic/poetic spelling -e ending). Стакнется is a 3rd-person singular future reflexive verb ('will collide/meet'), modified by когда-нибудь ('sometime/ever' formed from когда + нибудь) and совершенно ('completely'). С + рассудком uses the instrumental to indicate 'with/against reason' (here: 'with reason' / 'in relation to reason').
segments.p1-8-g73: Так...мы будем рассуждать expresses the result: так (in that case) + periphrastic future будeм + infinitive рассуждать ('we will reason/discuss'). The particles ведь and уж add colloquial emphasis/certainty ('of course/indeed'). Тогда repeats the temporal/resultive 'then'.
segments.p1-8-g74: А introduces contrast ('and/but'); не хотеть is a negation of the verb 'to want'. Собственно here nuances 'properly/strictly speaking' (marks the precise sense of 'to want'). Потому begins the causal connector потому, что ('because'), linking to the following clause.
segments.p1-8-g75: Что completes the потому, что ('because') clause. Нельзя is an impersonal modal ('one cannot/it is impossible'); the particles ведь and же add colloquial emphasis ('after all/indeed'), colouring the prohibition as obvious or emphatic.
segments.p1-8-g76: Например is an adverb meaning 'for example' and introduces the illustrative case that follows.
segments.p1-8-g77: Сохраняя is a деепричастие (present gerund) of сохранять meaning 'while preserving' or 'keeping'; it adverbially modifies the main verb and requires the same subject as the main clause. Рассудок is the object in the accusative (here: 'one's reason/sanity').
segments.p1-8-g78: Хотеть бессмыслицы: хотеть governs the desired object (бессмыслицы, genitive here) — 'to want nonsense'. И таким образом = 'and in this way/thus' (таким образом is an adverbial phrase). Зазнамо is an archaic/colloquial adverb 'knowingly/deliberately' modifying идти; идти против + genitive ('рассудка') means 'to go/act against reason'. И желать себе вредного: желать plus genitive ('вредного') means 'to desire/wish [something harmful]' and себе (dative) marks the beneficiary ('for oneself').
segments.p1-8-g81: А так как together form the causal conjunction 'and/since' (literally 'and so how'), introducing the subordinate clause. Все хотенья и рассуждения is a coordinated subject ('all desires and reasonings'). Могут быть + passive participle вычислены is a modal periphrasis meaning 'can be calculated' (present/future general possibility).
segments.p1-8-g82: Потому что is the causal 'because.' Когда-нибудь is an adverbial temporal with softening 'нибудь' meaning 'sometime/at some future time.' Откроют is 3rd-person plural future/simple perfective 'they will discover/reveal' and же is an emphatic particle. Законы here means general 'principles/rules.' Называемой is the passive participle used as 'so-called' modifying 'нашей свободной воли.'
segments.p1-8-g83: То is a consequence/continuative particle here ('then/so'), linking the previous subordinate clause to the speaker's inference.
segments.p1-8-g84: Стало быть is a fixed phrase meaning 'therefore/it follows that.' Grammatically it's past participle + infinitive of 'быть' but functions as a discourse connective rather than a literal past tense.
segments.p1-8-g85: И is the simple coordinating conjunction 'and,' continuing the sentence.
segments.p1-8-g86: Кроме шуток literally 'apart from jokes' is an idiom meaning 'no kidding' or 'seriously' and functions as a parenthetical adverbial.
segments.p1-8-g87: Может here is modal 'might/may.' Устроиться in the reflexive form means 'to come about / be arranged / turn out' (a resultative intransitive), so 'может устроиться' = 'something might come about.' Что-нибудь is the indefinite 'something,' and вроде таблички means 'something like a small plaque/chart' (вроде = 'sort of/like').
segments.p1-8-g88: Так что introduces the result clause 'so that.' Мы действительно хотеть будем is a future periphrasis with будем + infinitive meaning 'we will want' (будем is auxiliary future of 'быть'); действительно intensifies it ('indeed/actually'). По этой табличке uses the preposition по with dative to mean 'according to / by means of this (little) chart.'
segments.p1-8-g89: Ведь is a discourse particle 'after all/you know' adding emphasis to the conditional introduced by если. Мне is the dative 'to me' and begins a clause whose verbs will be impersonal/plural (they will calculate/prove to me).
segments.p1-8-g90: Например is an adverb meaning 'for example' and simply marks the following hypothetical as illustrative.
segments.p1-8-g91: Когда-нибудь combines когда ('when') with the particle-нибудь to mean 'sometime/ever' — an indefinite future/occasional time adverbial.
segments.p1-8-g92: Расчислят is a nonstandard/variant future 3rd-person plural form (≈ 'they will calculate/compute/settle'), followed by и докажут ('and they will prove'). Both verbs are future perfective actions performed by unspecified agents.
segments.p1-8-g93: Что introduces the content clause. Если я показал is a conditional clause 'if I showed' (показал = past tense of 'показать'); такому-то + то is an indeterminate/pejorative 'to such-and-such (person)' construction, and кукиш is the object meaning the fig-sign (a dismissive gesture).
segments.p1-8-g94: Так именно потому is a sequence of particles meaning 'for precisely that reason' (так = 'so/thus', именно = 'exactly', потому = 'because/for that reason'), signaling the causal explanation to follow.
segments.p1-8-g95: Что ... не мог не показать is a double negation idiom meaning 'that I could not but show' (i.e. 'I couldn't refrain from showing'). Непременно таким-то пальцем должен был его показать strings modals and auxiliaries: непременно = 'certainly/necessarily', таким-то пальцем = 'with that particular finger' (instrumental), должен был + infinitive = 'had to / must have' (obligation expressed in the past), and the final показать repeats the infinitive 'to show' (object его = 'it/him').
segments.p1-8-g96: Так что introduces the result 'so what then ... will remain?' Же is an emphatic particle; во мне = 'in me' (в with the strengthened form во before м-); свободного-то uses the adjective in the genitive (or nominative with pronominal emphasis) with a final -то particle adding a brusque/demonstrative tone; останется is future 'will remain/be left.'
segments.p1-8-g97: Особенно если introduces a reinforcing conditional 'especially if ...' Я ученый is a nominative predicate 'I am a scholar.' Где-нибудь курс наук кончил uses где-нибудь ('somewhere/at some place') and кончил (past tense 'finished/graduated') with курс наук = 'a course of studies/sciences' — together asking whether formal education would make the lack of freedom even clearer.
segments.p1-8-g98: Ведь is a discourse particle meaning “after all” that frames the remark. Я тогда sets the subject and temporal frame (“I, then/in that case”); вперед here means “in advance” and modifies the action. Всю мою жизнь (accusative) is the object “my whole life,” with the duration phrase на тридцать лет specifying how far ahead; рассчитать (infinitive) + могy (finite modal verb at the end) is a common Russian order meaning “can calculate.”
segments.p1-8-g99: Одним словом is an idiomatic summarising phrase formed with the instrumental одн-ым + словом, literally “with one word,” used like English “in short” or “in a word.” Grammatically it is a parenthetical discourse marker and not syntactically required by the clause that follows.
segments.p1-8-g100: Если introduces a conditional “if.” The particle и after если here intensifies the condition (“even if” / “even should”), not a simple copulative “and.” Устроится is the reflexive perfective 3rd-person-singular (будущее/результативное) of устроить/устроиться meaning “be arranged / work out”; это is the pronoun “this/it.”
segments.p1-8-g101: Так here is a result/so adverb (“then/in that case”), and ведь again emphasizes (“after all”). Нам is the dative (“to us”); уж is a particle meaning “already/indeed.” Нечего is an impersonal expression meaning “there is nothing (to…)” and is followed by будет делать, literally “there will be to do,” i.e. “there will be nothing to do.”
segments.p1-8-g102: Все равно is a fixed adverbial phrase meaning “all the same / anyway.” Надо будет is an impersonal modal construction where надо (“must/necessary”) combines with будущее будет to give a future necessity (“will have to”); принять is the infinitive “to accept.” Together: “it will still have to be accepted / we’ll have to accept it.”
segments.p1-8-g27: А is an introductory/contrastive particle here ('and/then' or 'well'); вы is the subject 'you' (formal or plural). тут is an adverb meaning 'at this point / here' used colloquially to mark the moment. и before the verb is an emphatic particle meaning 'even' or indicating suddenness. заговорили is the past tense (perfective) plural form of заговорить 'to begin to speak', agreeing with вы and marking the action as having begun.
segments.p1-8-g28: Ведь is a discourse particle 'after all' adding emphasis. в самом деле is an idiom ('in fact / really / indeed') formed by preposition в + emphatic сам + дело 'matter', used here as a parenthetical remark.
segments.p1-8-g29: ну is an interjection/filler 'well' or 'now', softening or pacing the sentence; it doesn't change grammar but signals the speaker's casual register.
segments.p1-8-g30: This is a conditional clause introduced by если 'if'. вправду 'truly' modifies the verb. найдут is the 3rd-person plural future of найти (perfective) 'will find/discover' (used impersonally for 'they/people/one will find'). когда-нибудь (когда + нибудь) means 'sometime/ever'. формулу всех наших хотений и капризов is the direct object: 'the formula of all our desires and whims.'
segments.p1-8-g31: то есть is the explanatory phrase 'that is' (то + есть, with есть functioning as the copula/clarifier). от чего они зависят is an indirect question 'what they depend on' (от + relative чего; зависят is present plural 'depend').
segments.p1-8-g32: по каким именно законам is a prepositional phrase 'by which exact laws' (по + dative plural законам), where именно reinforces exactness. происходят is present plural 'occur / originate', so the clause asks by what laws these wants originate.
segments.p1-8-g33: как именно распространяются is a short clause 'how exactly they spread/distribute' — как 'how' plus именно 'exactly', and распространяются is the present plural reflexive/passive-type verb 'are spread / are distributed'.
segments.p1-8-g34: куда стремятся 'where they tend/aim' uses куда 'to where' with стремятся present plural 'strive/aim/move toward'. в таком-то и в таком-то случае is a formulaic 'in such-and-such a case' with the emphatic particle -то attached (таком-то) to give vagueness; repeated structure lists examples. и проч. is an abbreviation of и прочее 'and so on / etc.'
segments.p1-8-g35: A simple punctuation mark (comma) separating this elliptical fragment from the preceding list; carries no lexical content.
segments.p1-8-g36: "и" is the coordinating conjunction 'and' linking this fragment to what came before; "проч." is an abbreviated colloquial form of "прочее/прочее́го" meaning 'etc.' or 'and the rest' and signals an elliptical continuation of the list.
segments.p1-8-g37: A comma marking the start of a parenthetical clarification that follows the previous clause.
segments.p1-8-g38: "То есть" is the fixed phrase 'that is' (то = 'that', есть = copula 'is') introducing a clarification; "настоящую математическую формулу" is a noun phrase in the accusative singular (настоящую, математическую are attributive adjectives modifying формулу) meaning 'a genuine/real mathematical formula.'
segments.p1-8-g39: "- так ведь тогда" is a colloquial sequence marking consequence and emphasis (так = 'then/in that case'; ведь = 'after all/you see'; тогда = 'then'); "человек тотчас же" is the subject plus adverbial 'a person immediately' (тотчас же = 'at once/immediately', with же adding emphasis).
segments.p1-8-g40: "пожалуй" is a modal particle meaning 'perhaps' or 'one might say', marking the speaker's tentativeness about the assertion.
segments.p1-8-g41: "и" is the coordinating conjunction 'and'; "перестанет" is the perfective future (3rd sing.) of "перестать" meaning 'will cease' or 'would cease' in this hypothetical context; it governs the infinitive "хотеть" ('to want'), so the phrase means 'and will cease to want.'
segments.p1-8-g42: "да еще" is a colloquial intensifier meaning 'and besides' or 'moreover' (да here functioning as a linking particle 'and'), adding an extra point to what was just said.
segments.p1-8-g43: Another "пожалуй", repeating the tentative stance ('perhaps') and softening the following claim.
segments.p1-8-g44: "и" again links clauses ('and'); "наверно" is an adverb meaning 'probably' or 'most likely'; "перестанет" repeats the perfective future 'will cease' with an omitted object (elliptical repetition referring back to the previous action), so the segment means roughly 'and will probably cease (as well).' The omission is stylistic, leaving the verb's complement understood from the prior clause.
segments.p1-8-g45: Ну is a discourse particle here meaning “well” that opens a rhetorical question. Что за охота is a fixed colloquial formula meaning “what sort of desire/urge” (что за + noun). Хотеть is an infinitive governed by охота (i.e. “a desire to do X”), and по + dative (табличке) means “according to / by means of” — “to want according to the little plate/chart.”
segments.p1-8-g46: Мало того is an idiomatic phrase meaning “moreover” or “not only that.” Here мало does not mean quantity but combines with того to signal an additional, emphatic point.
segments.p1-8-g47: Тотчас же (‘at once / immediately’) is an adverbial phrase (же adds emphasis). Обратится is 3rd person singular future of the perfective reflexive verb обратиться meaning “will turn/be turned into”; the verb precedes он (обратится он) for stylistic emphasis. Из человека в … expresses change of state (“from a man into …”), and вроде того = “or the like / or something of that sort.”
segments.p1-8-g48: Потому here is a conjunction/adverb meaning “for that reason / because,” introducing the explanation that follows. It functions like “because” linking the previous statement to the explanatory clause.
segments.p1-8-g49: Что же такое человек … is an exclamatory / rhetorical construction meaning “what (is) a man …” with the copula omitted (common in Russian). Же adds emphasis, такое (literally “such”) highlights the category being questioned, and человек is the noun being defined; без + genitive (желаний) means “without desires.”
segments.p1-8-g50: This segment repeats без (without) for each noun as a stylistic, emphatic negative list: без воли (without will) и без хотений (and without wishes/wants). Both воля and хотение are in the genitive after без; хотений is a plural nominal form meaning desires/wants.
segments.p1-8-g51: Как не …? is a rhetorical tag meaning roughly “if not …?” or “what else is he but …?” Не negates the noun that follows, making the simile: штифтик (a small peg/pin). В + locative here gives the setting (“in the organ [mechanism]’s shaft/roller”); органном is the adjective “organ-related.” The whole phrase asks whether a man deprived of will and desires is anything but a peg in an organ mechanism.
segments.p1-8-g52: "Как вы думаете?" is a standard interrogative: "Как" here means 'how' or idiomatically 'what' in the sense of asking for the listener's opinion; "вы" is the polite/plural 'you'; "думаете" is the 2nd-person present indicative of "думать" ('think'). The literal 'How do you think?' is equivalent to English 'What do you think?'.
segments.p1-8-g53: Сосчитаем is 1st-person plural of the perfective verb 'сосчитать' used here hortatively — a 'let's' construction (literally 'we will/count'). It takes the plural noun 'вероятности' as its direct object; word order Verb + Object is neutral and idiomatic.
segments.p1-8-g54: Может is the impersonal/modal use of 'мочь' (= 'perhaps/maybe'), not literal ability; it governs the infinitive случиться. The pronoun это follows the modal and is the subject of the infinitive. The clause ends with the tag 'или нет?' ('or not?') to form a yes/no question.
segments.p1-8-g55: Interjection representing a vocal hesitation or thoughtful sound; here it stands alone as the entire utterance, equivalent to English "Hmm."
segments.p1-8-g58: "Решаете вы" is present-tense, 2nd-person plural indicative of "решать" (to decide/conclude). The pronoun "вы" follows the verb for emphasis (inversion) and the dash marks it as reported speech or an interlocutor's remark.
segments.p1-8-g59: The subject "наши хотенья" (our desires) is followed by the phrase "большею частию бывают ошибочны" — "большею частию" is an older adverbial phrase meaning "for the most part"; "бывают" is the plural habitual/copular use of "бывать" and links the subject to the predicate adjective "ошибочны" (short-form adjective "mistaken"). The prepositional phrase "от ошибочного взгляда на наши выгоды" uses "от" + genitive to indicate cause/reason ("because of a mistaken view"), and "взгляд на" takes "на" + accusative to mark what the view is directed at.
segments.p1-8-g60: "Мы" is the plural first-person subject. "Потому" here links to the previous remark meaning "for that reason/therefore." The particle "и" functions as an intensifier/connector. "Хотим" is present-tense plural of "хотеть" (we want). "Иногда" is an adverb "sometimes." "Чистого вздору" is an older-styled object phrase: "чистого" (pure/absolute) modifies "вздору" (genitive form of "вздор" used after "хотеть" in this collocation), meaning "pure nonsense."
segments.p1-8-g61: "Что" introduces the content clause ("that/because"). "В этом вздоре" is a locative phrase formed with "в" + demonstrative "этом" + noun "вздоре," meaning "in this nonsense." "Видим" is present-tense plural of "видеть," here meaning "we see/perceive." The clause links as the reason explaining why we sometimes want nonsense.
segments.p1-8-g62: "По глупости нашей" is an adverbial phrase meaning "through/out of our stupidity" or "because of our stupidity." The preposition "по" with the noun in the (here) genitive or dative-like form expresses the cause or circumstance; "глупости" is the noun "stupidity" and "нашей" is the possessive adjective "our" agreeing with it.
segments.p1-8-g63: "Легчайшую дорогу" is the accusative (direct-object) phrase: superlative adjective "легчайшую" agrees with feminine noun "дорогу" meaning "the easiest way/path." "К достижению" uses the preposition "к" + dative to mean "toward the attainment of." "Какой-нибудь" is an indefinite modifier "some/any," softened by the particle "-нибудь." "Заранее предположенной выгоды" is a participial construction: "заранее" (in advance) modifies the past-participle "предположенной" (assumed/presumed), which together modify "выгоды" (advantage), all governed by "к достижению".
segments.p1-8-g64: Interjection "Ну" here marks a small transition or resumption of speech, equivalent to English "Well..."
segments.p1-8-g65: "А когда все это будет растолковано" contains the conjunction/contrastive particle "а" (but/and), the temporal "когда" (when) and the subject "все это" (all this). The verb phrase "будет растолковано" is a future passive construction: auxiliary "будет" + past passive participle "растолковано," meaning "will be explained/interpreted."
segments.p1-8-g66: "Расчислено на бумажке" continues the list of results: "расчислено" is a short/passive participle meaning "calculated/worked out," and "на бумажке" indicates the medium "on paper" (diminutive/colloquial "бумажка" = piece of paper). The parenthetical clause begins with "что" introducing a remark about possibility.
segments.p1-8-g67: "Потому что" introduces the reason: "because." "Гнусно же и бессмысленно заранее верить" is a predicative phrase: "гнусно" (vile/base) and "бессмысленно" (senseless/pointless) are neuter predicates describing the infinitive "верить" (to believe). The particle "же" adds emphasis.
segments.p1-8-g68: This clause "что иных законов природы человек никогда не узнает" is the content of "верить": "that man will never learn other laws of nature." "Иных" modifies "законов" (genitive plural), "природы" is a genitive attributive (laws of nature), "человек" is the subject and "никогда не узнает" is the future-tense verb phrase with negation (will never learn/come to know).
segments.p1-8-g69: "То тогда" is a colloquial reinforcing pair meaning "then/in that case"; "то" functions as a particle intensifying the temporal "тогда."
segments.p1-8-g70: "Разумеется" is an adverb meaning "of course" or "naturally," commenting on the expected result.
segments.p1-8-g71: "Не будет" is the future-negative impersonal construction meaning "there will not be." After this negation the noun is put in genitive: "так называемых желаний" — "так называемых" is a passive participial adjective meaning "so-called" modifying "желаний" (desires). The whole phrase means "there will be no so-called desires."
segments.p1-8-g125: A direct vocative/nominative address to the audience: a polite way to say "gentlemen." In 19th‑century Russian this plural form is used to get attention or address a group.
segments.p1-8-g126: Polite imperative phrased to those addressed: вы (you, plural or formal) + меня (direct object) + извините (imperative, "excuse/forgive"). The speaker asks pardon for what follows.
segments.p1-8-g127: A content clause introduced by что ("that") with subject я (I) and the perfective/reflexive past verb зафилософствовался meaning 'got to philosophizing' or 'began philosophizing' — here it conveys having indulged in philosophical talk.
segments.p1-8-g128: Телеметрический/time/location phrase: тут ("here/at this point") + numeric duration сорок лет ("forty years") + подполья in the genitive to indicate the kind of time/condition ('of the underground'). The construction expresses duration/character — 'forty years of underground life.'
segments.p1-8-g129: Imperative polite позвольте (addressed to вы) followed by an infinitive; пофантазировать is a prefixed infinitive (по-) that implies a brief or indulgent action: 'to indulge in a bit of fantasizing.'
segments.p1-8-g130: A colloquial discourse opener: видите ли is 'you see'/'you know'; here the author adds an emphatic/colloquial particle written -с (not a preposition) to imitate spoken style. The clause prepares an explanation that follows.
segments.p1-8-g131: Noun in the nominative: рассудок ('reason') as the topic/subject being discussed; it will be repeated for rhetorical emphasis.
segments.p1-8-g132: Another direct address (vocative/nominative): 'господа' (gentlemen), inserted as a parenthetical to keep the addressees in view while the argument proceeds.
segments.p1-8-g133: Copular clause with existential/useful copula есть ('is'): вещь хорошая is a noun + adjective phrase meaning 'a good thing.' The word order (noun before adjective) is emphatic/colloquial but fully grammatical.
segments.p1-8-g134: Это ('this/it') plus the predicative adverb бесспорно ('indisputably') — a short parenthetical comment reinforcing the previous claim; here бесспорно functions like 'no doubt/undeniably.'
segments.p1-8-g135: Contrast clause introduced by но ('but'): the sentence repeats рассудок with the copula есть and the particle только ('only') for emphasis: 'reason is only reason.' The second half uses и (and) + удовлетворяет (3rd person singular present 'satisfies') with только again to limit the object: 'satisfies only the rational capacity of a person' (способности человека is genitive, 'capacity of a person').
segments.p1-8-g136: А ('whereas') introduces the counterpoint: хотенье (archaised noun 'wanting'/'desire') + есть + проявление всей жизни — 'desire is the manifestation of the whole of life.' Всей governs жизни (genitive) so the phrase means 'of all life.'
segments.p1-8-g137: То есть introduces an apposition/explanation ('that is') restating the idea more narrowly: 'that is, of all human life.' Человеческой is an adjective in the genitive agreeing with жизни.
segments.p1-8-g138: И с рассудком — a short prepositional phrase in the instrumental (с + рассудком) meaning 'with reason.' The союз и here links this item into the list of what desire includes.
segments.p1-8-g139: И со всеми почесываниями — continuation of the list: со is a phonetic variant of с used before the pronoun all (всеми); почесываниями is instrumental plural of почесывание, literal 'scratchings' used figuratively as 'little urges/itches.' The whole phrase means 'and with all its little itchings.'
segments.p1-8-g140: Concessive clause: И хоть ('and although') + subject жизнь наша ('our life') + adverbial phrase в этом проявлении ('in this manifestation'). The verb выходит (3rd sing. present) here means 'turns out'/'results' and is followed by the colloquial predicative дрянцо ('rotten/awful') and the frequency adverb зачастую ('often').
segments.p1-8-g141: Contrastive pivot: но все-таки ('but still/after all') is a fixed concessive particle meaning 'nevertheless' or 'all the same'; followed by the noun жизнь repeated for emphasis — 'but still, life.'
segments.p1-8-g142: Contrastive negation: а не ('and not') + одно только ('only a single') modifying извлечение квадратного корня ('the extraction of a square root') — a metaphorical phrase meaning mere cold calculation. Квадратного is the genitive adjective agreeing with корня (genitive), so the whole phrase is a noun phrase in apposition: 'not just the mere extraction of a square root.'
segments.p1-8-g103: "Да и вообще мы должны," is a discursive opening: "Да и" functions like 'and/besides', "вообще" means 'in general', and "мы должны" is a modal construction 'we ought/should'. Word order is standard subject + predicate; "должны" is a present-tense modal adjective used as 'must/ought to.'
segments.p1-8-g104: "не уставая," is a gerund phrase (деепричастие) formed from "уставать" with negation; it means 'without getting tired' or 'without ceasing'. It modifies the main verb phrase that follows and is linked adverbially to the obligation in the previous segment.
segments.p1-8-g105: "повторять себе," is an infinitive + dative/reflexive phrase: "повторять" here means 'to repeat/reiterate' and "себе" ('to ourselves') indicates the internal, reflexive object. It's the direct object of the obligation in the earlier clause (we ought to do this).
segments.p1-8-g106: This whole clause begins with the complementizer "что" ('that') introducing reported content; "непременно" is an adverb 'certainly', and the time/place phrases "в такую-то минуту и в таких-то обстоятельствах" use "в" in a temporal sense ('at/during') with demonstrative "такую-то/таких-то" ('such-and-such' — the particle "-то" adds vagueness/emphasis). The predicate "природа нас не спрашивается" is idiomatic: literally passive-ish but meaning 'nature does not consult/ask us' (i.e. doesn't ask our permission).
segments.p1-8-g107: "что нужно принимать ее так," continues the reported content introduced by "что": "нужно" is an impersonal/modal adjective meaning 'it is necessary' or 'one must', "принимать" is the infinitive 'to accept', and "ее" (referring back to "природа") is the accusative object 'it/her'. "так" ('so/as such') indicates manner.
segments.p1-8-g108: "как она есть," is a standard simile/relational clause: "как" = 'as', "она" = the feminine pronoun referring to the thing to be accepted, and "есть" is the present infinitive/old-style present of 'to be' meaning 'is' — together 'as it is'.
segments.p1-8-g109: "а не так," is a short contrastive clause: "а" = 'but', negation "не" plus "так" = 'that way' or 'so', contrasting acceptance-as-is with the alternative that follows.
segments.p1-8-g110: "как мы фантазируем," is another 'как' clause ('as/like'), with subject "мы" and finite verb "фантазируем" (present tense 'we fantasize'/'we imagine'). It expresses the contrasting way in which we tend to imagine things, not the way they are.
segments.p1-8-g111: "и если мы действительно стремимся к табличке и к календарю," is a conditional clause introduced by "если" ('if'). "действительно" is an adverb 'indeed/actually', "стремимся" is present-tense 'we strive/aspire', and "к табличке и к календарю" are dative phrases 'toward a little plaque and toward the calendar' (objects/goals of striving).
segments.p1-8-g112: "ну," is a modal/discourse particle 'well', used to soften or lead into what follows; it's an interjection with no syntactic role here.
segments.p1-8-g113: "и." is a trailing conjunction 'and' used here to trailing-off the sentence — colloquial ellipsis: the speaker breaks off, leaving the thought unfinished.
segments.p1-8-g116: "ну хоть бы даже и к реторте," is a concessive clause: "ну" = 'well', "хоть бы даже" is an emphatic concessive phrase 'even if/let's say even', "и" is a colloquial additive particle, and "к реторте" is a dative phrase 'to the retort' (retort = laboratory vessel used figuratively here).
segments.p1-8-g117: "то что же делать," is a colloquial rhetorical question: "то" functions as 'then/so', "что же делать" = 'what (then) is to be done?', where "же" adds emphasis/urgency and "делать" is the infinitive 'to do'.
segments.p1-8-g118: "надо принять и реторту!" is an impersonal obligation: "надо" = 'one must/it is necessary', "принять" = 'to accept', and "и реторту" = 'even the retort' (accusative). The exclamation marks the resigned inevitability.
segments.p1-8-g119: Не то at the start of a clause functions as a contrastive connective meaning “otherwise” or “or else.” Она is the feminine pronoun/subject (“she/it”), and сама is an intensive reflexive meaning “herself/itself” or “on its own,” emphasizing that the action will happen without outside help.
segments.p1-8-g120: Без + вас is the standard preposition + genitive “without you.” Примется is the 3rd-person-singular future of приняться (reflexive), meaning “will begin / will take to (something),” here “will start/take hold.” The clause is a simple future statement about an action occurring independently.
segments.p1-8-g123: Да‑с is an informal/exclamatory interjection: Да “yes” plus a clipped particle s indicating hesitation or emphasis; it functions as a conversational “yes, well…” It is not a two-word literal preposition phrase here but an exclamatory token.
segments.p1-8-g124: Но is the adversative conjunction “but.” Вот and тут together point to the current spot (“here/at this point”); the enclitic -то on тут (тут‑то) adds emphasis: “precisely here.” Для меня is a dative phrase “for me,” and и before запятая functions as a linking/emphatic particle (“and/indeed”), while запятая literally means “comma” but here is used figuratively for a pause or sticking point — “the catch/the comma.”
segments.p1-8-g225: Неблагодарен is a short-form adjective used predicatively, equivalent to “(is) ungrateful”; the subject is implied from the prior sentence (he/one/human). Феноменально is an adverb modifying the predicate and gives emphatic degree (“phenomenally/remarkably”), placed after the predicate for emphasis.
segments.p1-8-g226: Я думаю is a simple present 1st-person statement (“I think”); даже is an adverb of emphasis (“even”), inserted for force. The comma marks that this is the lead-in to a subordinate clause.
segments.p1-8-g227: Что introduces a content clause (“that …”). Самое лучшее forms a superlative nominal phrase (“the very best”); here самое intensifies лучшее. Определение человека is the noun phrase “definition of man/people.” The dash and colon that follow set up the explicit apposition/definition that comes next.
segments.p1-8-g228: Существо is a neuter noun “a creature/being.” На двух ногах is a locative prepositional phrase meaning “on two legs” (на + plural form), giving a physical characteristic. И неблагодарное coordinates a second characteristic (“and ungrateful”); неблагодарное is a full adjective in nominative neuter agreeing with существо and functioning as a predicate/adjectival attribute.
segments.p1-8-g229: Но это ещё не всё is an idiomatic clause meaning “But this is not all.” Ещё here = “yet/still,” and всё = “everything/that’s all”; the word order is the typical Russian predicate construction with это as the demonstrative subject.
segments.p1-8-g230: Это ещё не главный недостаток его repeats the demonstrative subject это with the negated predicate “is not the main defect.” Главный modifies недостаток (“main/chief defect”), and его is a possessive pronoun placed after the noun (colloquial placement) meaning “his.”
segments.p1-8-g231: Главнейший недостаток его - это постоянное неблагонравие uses главнейший (superlative/stronger form of главный) + недостаток to state “his chiefest defect.” The dash introduces an explanatory apposition: это + noun phrase (постоянное неблагонравие), where постоянное is an adjective modifying the abstract noun неблагонравие (“immorality/indecency”) used as the predicate.
segments.p1-8-g232: Постоянное here is repeated and set off by a comma for rhetorical emphasis (“constant, …”). Grammatically it’s the same adjective in nominative neuter agreeing with the abstract noun неблагонравие.
segments.p1-8-g233: Начиная от … до … is a free participial/gerundial construction meaning “beginning from … up to …,” introducing a chronological range. От and до take nouns in the genitive (Всемирного потопа, Шлезвиг-Гольштейнского периода); судеб человеческих is a genitive noun phrase “of human fates/destinies,” where человеческих is the modifying adjective in genitive plural.
segments.p1-8-g234: Неблагонравие in nominative functions as the subject/topic of the sentence (“Immorality…”). The single noun set off by a comma serves as the opening idea to be connected to what follows.
segments.p1-8-g235: А следственно is a connective phrase: а = “and/but” linking clauses, and следственно is an adverb meaning “consequently/therefore,” signaling that the next noun follows as a consequence.
segments.p1-8-g236: И неблагоразумие uses и to add “and,” naming the related consequence “neblagorazumie” (imprudence/indiscretion) as another resulting quality. The semicolon separates this coordinated pair from the explanatory clause that follows.
segments.p1-8-g237: Ибо is a formal conjunction meaning “for/because,” introducing the reason; давно известно is an impersonal construction meaning “it has long been known” or “long ago it became known,” where давно indicates duration (“for a long time”) and известно is the impersonal predicate (“is known”).
segments.p1-8-g238: Что introduces the content clause (“that …”). Неблагоразумие is the subject of the subordinate clause. Не иначе происходит is a fixed negated phrase meaning “does not happen otherwise” or “cannot occur except,” where не + иначе (“not otherwise”) modifies происходит (present tense of происходить) meaning “originates/occurs.”
segments.p1-8-g239: Как от неблагонравия completes the idiom не иначе … как от, meaning “except/from” — i.e. “only from immorality.” От takes the genitive (неблагонравия) as the source/cause.
segments.p1-8-g240: Попробуйте is the 2nd-person plural/formal imperative of попробовать ('try'), used to invite the reader to attempt something. The particle же adds emphasis/insistence. Бросьте (imperative of бросить) pairs with взгляд (direct object) to form the phrase бросьте взгляд 'cast/throw a glance'. На + accusative (историю) marks the target of the glance: 'at the history'.
segments.p1-8-g241: Ну is an interjection meaning 'well' or 'now' used here to prompt or soften the following question; it does not change grammatical structure but signals a transition to the interrogative.
segments.p1-8-g242: Что introduces a direct question 'what'. Вы is the 2nd-person plural/formal pronoun. Увидите is the future tense (perfective verb увидеть) 2nd-person plural, asking for the result of the suggested action: 'what will you see?'.
segments.p1-8-g243: Величественно is an adverb 'majestically' used elliptically as a one-word question; it functions as a predicative adverb asking whether the thing in question has that quality.
segments.p1-8-g244: Пожалуй is a modal particle meaning 'perhaps' or 'it would seem', used to soften or hedge the statement that follows; it sets a tentative tone.
segments.p1-8-g245: Хоть и is a concessive construction meaning 'though' or 'even if' (хоть + particle и for emphasis). Величественно is the adjective/adverb 'majestic(ally)' being conceded: 'though (it is) majestic'.
segments.p1-8-g246: Уж here is an emphatic particle (roughly 'indeed' / 'just') stressing the following example. Колосс Родосский is a nominal phrase 'the Colossus of Rhodes' (Родосский specifies which colossus); word order is the normal noun + adjective/attributive.
segments.p1-8-g247: Например is a parenthetical adverb 'for example', marking that what follows is an illustrative instance.
segments.p1-8-g248: Чего стоит! is an exclamatory idiom: чего is the interrogative/exclamatory element ('what'/'how'), and стоит is from стоить 'to be worth' (here 'is worth' or 'means a great deal'); together they produce the exclamation 'what it is worth!' or 'think of that!'.
segments.p1-8-g249: Недаром is an adverbial phrase 'not without reason' introducing a justificatory statement. Же is emphatic. Г-н Анаевский is the subject ('Mr. Anaevsky'); свидетельствует is present tense, 3rd-person singular 'testifies' or 'attests'. О + нем is the prepositional object 'about it'.
segments.p1-8-g250: Что here introduces reported speech ('that'). Одни is an indefinite plural meaning 'some (people)' and functions as the subject of говорят; говорят is present tense 'say' or 'are saying'—a generic report of opinion.
segments.p1-8-g251: Будто introduces an indirect clause meaning 'as if' or 'that' in reported speech; он is the pronoun 'it/he'. Есть is the present copula (here stylistically explicit) 'is'. Произведение рук человеческих is an idiomatic genitive phrase 'a work/product of human hands' (произведение = work/artistic product; рук = 'of hands', human agency; человеческих = 'human').
segments.p1-8-g252: Другие же is the contrasting clause-initial phrase 'others, however' (другие = 'others', же = contrastive/emphatic). Утверждают is present tense 'assert' or 'maintain', introducing the opposing view.
segments.p1-8-g253: Что introduces the content of the assertion. Он is 'it'. Создан is a short passive participle (perfective) meaning 'created' or 'was created'. Самою природою uses the instrumental to mark the agent/means: самою (intensive 'by the very') + природою (archaic/instrumental form of природа) = 'by nature itself'.
segments.p1-8-g213: "и" is a discourse-linking 'and' here; "знаете" is the 2nd-person-plural/polite present of знать ('to know' / 'are aware'); the enclitic "ли" turns the phrase into a question (softening it to 'do you know...?'). Together the three form a colloquial introductory question.
segments.p1-8-g214: "что" introduces the subordinate clause ('that'); "и это" literally 'and/also this' (= 'this too'); "полезно" is a neuter predicative (short/adverbial form) meaning 'is useful'; the following "и даже иногда очень похвально" piles on modifiers: "и" = and, "даже" = 'even', "иногда" = 'sometimes', "очень" = 'very', and "похвально" is another neuter predicative/adverbial meaning 'praiseworthy', so the whole clause states a predication about 'this'.
segments.p1-8-g215: "Господа" is a vocative plural addressing the audience ('gentlemen' / 'sirs') and functions as a direct form of address.
segments.p1-8-g216: "положим" is a conventional hortative/imperative-like use of положить meaning 'let us suppose' or 'assume' (first-person-plural idiomatically used to introduce a hypothesis).
segments.p1-8-g217: "что" introduces the content clause ('that'); "человек" is a generic noun 'a person/man' used generally; "не глуп" uses the short-form adjective глуп with negation to form a predicate 'is not stupid' (short-form adjectives commonly form the predicate in Russian).
segments.p1-8-g218: "Действительно" is an introductory adverb meaning 'indeed' or 'actually', marking agreement or emphasis with the following remark; here it begins a parenthetical comment.
segments.p1-8-g219: The impersonal phrase "никак нельзя сказать" means 'one cannot in any way say'; "нельзя" is an impersonal modal ('it is impossible' / 'cannot') governing the infinitive "сказать"; "этого" is the genitive of "это" (object 'this'), and "про него" is a prepositional phrase 'about him'.
segments.p1-8-g220: "хоть бы по тому одному" is an idiomatic concessive expression meaning 'if only for that one reason' or 'even for that single reason'; "хоть" here = 'at least/even', "бы" is the modal/conditional particle that softens the phrase, "по" marks the reason ('for'), and "тому одному" stresses 'that one particular [reason]'.
segments.p1-8-g221: "что" introduces the explanatory clause; "если уж он будет глуп" is a conditional ('if indeed he were to be stupid') where "если" = 'if', "уж" adds emphasis/irony ('indeed'/'already'), "он будет" is future indicative 'he will be', and "глуп" is the short-form adjective 'stupid'.
segments.p1-8-g222: "так" links to the preceding conditional as 'then/in that case'; "ведь" reinforces 'after all'; "кто же тогда будет умен?" is a rhetorical question ('who then would be clever?') with "же" as an emphatic particle and "умен" the short-form adjective 'clever/intelligent' in the predicate position.
segments.p1-8-g223: "Но" = 'but'; the concessive clause "если и не глуп" ('if even not stupid') uses "если" = 'if', "и" here = 'even' (a concessive particle), and the short-form adjective "глуп" forms the negated predicate 'not stupid'.
segments.p1-8-g224: The correlative "то" marks the result ('then'); "все-таки" (split here as "все" + "таки") is an adverbial phrase meaning 'nevertheless' or 'still'; "чудовищно" is an adverbial intensifier 'monstrously/extremely'; and "неблагодарен" is the short-form adjective predicate 'ungrateful'.
segments.p1-8-g287: A very short colloquial question. И is a discourse particle here meaning 'and/so' that links this question to the previous remark; что is the interrogative 'what'; ж (colloquial variant of же) is an emphatic particle adding the shade 'then/after all' — together forming the idiom 'И что ж?' = 'And what, then?'.
segments.p1-8-g288: Известно is an impersonal predicative often translated "it is known" or "it is well known." Here it opens the sentence and sets a general, detached statement; no explicit subject is required.
segments.p1-8-g289: многие из этих любителей is a noun phrase: многие (plural adjective/pronoun) + из (preposition 'of') + этих (demonstrative in genitive plural) + любителей (noun in genitive plural). The construction means "many of these enthusiasts/amateurs."
segments.p1-8-g290: рано ли is a short parenthetical expression meaning "whether early" or "sooner (or)"; ли is the enclitic particle forming a loose "whether/if" contrast with поздо ли that follows. Word order places the temporal adverb before ли for emphasis.
segments.p1-8-g291: поздно ли mirrors the previous segment: поздно (adverb) + ли (enclitic) giving the complementary sense "or late". The pair рано ли, поздно ли forms the idiom "sooner or later."
segments.p1-8-g292: под конец жизни is a temporal prepositional phrase: под + конец (noun in accusative) + жизни (genitive) meaning "toward the end of life." изменяли себе is the imperfective past tense plural of изменять + reflexive себе: here "they betrayed themselves" or "they transgressed/changed themselves"; the verb is used in the sense of "betray/deviate from" (not merely "change").
segments.p1-8-g293: произведя какой-нибудь анекдот is a gerund (деепричастие) phrase: произведя (perfective gerund of произвести) indicates a completed action that accompanies the main verb; какой-нибудь is an indefinite modifier meaning "some/any," and анекдот is the direct object. Grammatically the gerund gives the means: "having produced some anecdote."
segments.p1-8-g294: иногда даже из самых неприличнейших is a final adverbial/detail: иногда даже ("sometimes even") + из + самых + неприличнейших (genitive plural superlative) meaning "sometimes even one taken from the most indecent (ones)." The genitive after из marks source/origin.
segments.p1-8-g295: Теперь вас спрошу is the main clause: Теперь (now) + вас (accusative 'you') placed before the verb for emphasis + спрошу (1st person singular future perfective of спросить) meaning "I will ask (you)." The colon introduces the question that follows.
segments.p1-8-g296: чего же можно ожидать от человека как от существа is an impersonal interrogative construction: чего (genitive of что) is the object of ожидать; же is an emphatic particle; можно ожидать is an impersonal modal "can/ought to be expected"; от человека как от существа repeats от to specify the source/subject and uses как ('as') to mean "as a" or "in the capacity of."
segments.p1-8-g297: одаренного такими странными качествами is a participial phrase (short/attributive passive participle in genitive) modifying существа: одаренного (from одарить) agrees in case and marks a quality "endowed with." такими странными качествами uses the instrumental case after одарённый (одарённый чем) to express what the being is endowed with.
segments.p1-8-g298: Да осыпьте его всеми земными благами is an imperative exhortation: Да here functions as an optative particle ("go on/let/indeed") intensifying the command; осыпьте is the perfective imperative plural of осыпать "shower/cover with"; его is the object; всеми земными благами is instrumental plural indicating means/what he is to be showered with.
segments.p1-8-g299: утопите в счастье совсем с головой continues the imperative: утопите (imperative plural of утопить) is used figuratively here "drown/immerse (in happiness)." в счастье is the prepositional phrase "in happiness;" совсем с головой is an idiom meaning "utterly/completely," where с головой (literally 'with the head') is adverbial.
segments.p1-8-g300: так is a short adverb linking to the purpose clause that follows: "so/thus" and preparing the result expressed by the next subordinate clause.
segments.p1-8-g301: чтобы только пузырьки вскакивали на поверхности счастья is a subordinate clause of result/purpose: чтобы introduces the clause; только limits it ("only"); пузырьки (nominative plural) is the subject of вскакивали (imperfective past/plural form used after чтобы to express the desired habitual/resulting action); на поверхности счастья is a locative/genitive phrase "on the surface of happiness."
segments.p1-8-g302: как на воде is a simile: как ('as/like') + на воде ('on water') — a simple comparative phrase illustrating how the bubbles appear.
segments.p1-8-g303: дайте ему такое экономическое довольство is another imperative: дайте (imperative of дать) + ему (dative 'to him') + такое экономическое довольство (such + adjective + noun) — "give him such economic/contentment." The adjective экономическое qualifies довольство.
segments.p1-8-g304: чтоб ему совсем уж ничего больше не оставалось делать is a subordinate clause of consequence introduced by чтоб (colloquial form of чтобы). ему is dative ("for him"), совсем уж is a double intensifier ("absolutely/utterly"), ничего больше не оставалось делать is an impersonal-style construction meaning "nothing would remain for him to do any more." Note the use of оставалось (imperfective past) in this subjunctive-like result clause.
segments.p1-8-g305: кроме как спать is a short exception clause: кроме (except) + как (used here as "but/except to") + спать (infinitive) meaning "except to sleep." The construction "кроме как + infinitive" expresses the only remaining action.
segments.p1-8-g306: кушать пряники и хлопотать о непрекращении всемирной истории continues the list of remaining actions: кушать пряники (infinitive + direct object) "to eat gingerbread" and хлопотать о (to be busy/concern oneself about) + noun in prepositional/genitive; непрекращении is a noun meaning "unceasingness," and всемирной истории is genitive/attribute "of world history."
segments.p1-8-g307: - так он вам и тут, is a parenthetical return to the narrator's comment: так (so/then) + он (he) + вам (dative 'to you') + и (and/even) + тут (here/at this point). The dash marks a shift back to the narrator's voice and the colloquial "and even here" tone.
segments.p1-8-g308: человек-то is a nominative noun with the particle -то attached to add contrastive emphasis: человек (man/person) + -то (intensifier/contrast marker) roughly "the man himself/that fellow."
segments.p1-8-g309: и тут repeats the locative/emphatic phrase: и (and/even) + тут (here/at that point) strengthening "even then/there." It's a colloquial reinforcement of the previous point.
segments.p1-8-g310: из одной неблагодарности is a prepositional phrase giving motive: из (from/out of) + одной (one, genitive feminine) + неблагодарности (genitive noun 'ingratitude') meaning "out of a single act of ingratitude" or "from sheer ingratitude."
segments.p1-8-g311: из одного пасквиля мерзость сделает is the concluding clause: из одного пасквиля ("out of/from a single pasquinade/lampoon" – пасквиля is genitive) gives the source; мерзость (nominative) is the thing he will produce; сделает (future/present perfective 3rd singular of сделать) means "will make/produce." Word order places the source first for emphasis: "out of one lampoon he will make an abomination."
segments.p1-8-g164: Simple main clause: Я (subject) + подозреваю (1st-person singular present imperfective verb 'I suspect'). The comma and vocative that follow mark a parenthetical address. The verb is present tense and expresses the speaker's current belief.
segments.p1-8-g165: A brief vocative address: господа is the plural form used to address the audience ('gentlemen'). It stands alone after a comma and does not change the sentence's syntax.
segments.p1-8-g166: Subordinate content clause introduced by что ('that') with the finite clause вы смотрите на меня с сожалением. смотреть here is a normal imperfective verb in present tense meaning 'to look (at)'; на governs the accusative меня. с сожалением is an adverbial phrase 'with pity/with regret' (с + instrumental).
segments.p1-8-g167: Continuation: вы повторяете мне — 'you repeat to me' (повторяете is 2nd-person plural present imperfective). мне is the dative 'to me' marking the indirect object. This introduces the content of what they keep saying.
segments.p1-8-g168: The embedded clause begins with что ('that') and contains a negated modal construction: не может (не + может) meaning 'cannot' or 'is not able to'. Просвещенный и развитой человек is a noun phrase with two past-participial/adj. modifiers (просвещённый 'enlightened', развитой 'developed') describing человек.
segments.p1-8-g169: Одним словом is an idiomatic parenthetical ('in a word' / 'in short'). Here словом is in the instrumental case following одним and functions as an adverbial parenthesis summarizing the preceding description.
segments.p1-8-g170: Такой is a demonstrative/pronominal adjective 'such' referring back to the described person; it stands before the relative clause that defines that kind of person.
segments.p1-8-g171: Relative clause: каким будет будущий человек — каким is the relative/formal pronoun 'such as/which', будет is future of быть ('will be'), and будущий человек is the noun phrase 'the future man'. The clause defines 'такой' by specifying the sort of person meant.
segments.p1-8-g172: The verb phrase uses an adverbial modifier зазнамо ('out of conceit, arrogantly') before the perfective infinitive захотеть ('to come to want/decide to want') — зазнамо modifies the manner of the wanting. Чего-нибудь (чего + нибудь) is an indefinite pronoun 'anything' (чего is here the genitive/partitive form used with захотеть), and для себя uses для + genitive to indicate 'for oneself'. Невыгодного is the genitive singular adjective agreeing with the omitted noun (something disadvantageous).
segments.p1-8-g173: Closes the speaker's report: что это математика — 'that this is mathematics.' Что introduces the reported clause; это is the demonstrative pronoun 'this/it', and математика is a predicate noun. The copula is omitted (typical in Russian) but understood: 'it is mathematics.'
segments.p1-8-g174: Short elliptical utterance: Совершенно (adverb) modifies the short-form adjective согласен, which functions as a predicate meaning 'I agree.' In Russian, short-form adjectival predicates are commonly used for 'I am X' statements.
segments.p1-8-g175: Действительно here is an adverb 'indeed' or 'really' and математика is a noun used as a nominal predicate; the copula 'is' is omitted, so the phrase reads 'indeed, (it is) mathematics.' This is an elliptical confirmation of the previous clause.
segments.p1-8-g176: Но повторяю вам в сотый раз is an emphatic opening: повторяю (1st-person present) 'I repeat to you' with вам in the dative. В сотый раз is an idiomatic time expression ('for the hundredth time'); ordinal + раз with в expresses iteration (how many times).
segments.p1-8-g177: Есть один только случай uses есть as an existential 'there is' (lit. 'is') plus один только случай 'one single case' (только intensifies один). Here есть does not mark past or future but existence/presence of such a case.
segments.p1-8-g178: Только один repeats and reinforces the exclusivity — an emphatic apposition: 'only one.' This duplication (один / только) is rhetorical for emphasis.
segments.p1-8-g179: Когда introduces the subordinate temporal/conditional clause 'when.' Человек (indefinite 'a person') is the clause's subject; может is the modal 'can/is able to' (present) and нарочно is an adverb 'deliberately/specially' modifying the verb phrase.
segments.p1-8-g180: Сознательно (another adverb) + пожелать (perfective infinitive 'to wish for') form the verb complex 'can consciously wish for.' Пожелать takes a dative indirect object (себе 'for himself') and then an object in the genitive when it's indefinite (here even harmful — вредного) — Russian often uses genitive after verbs of wanting for an indefinite or partitive sense.
segments.p1-8-g181: Глупого is one item in a series of adjectival/nominal objects in the genitive (вредного, глупого, ...). The list continues the idea of things one might wish for; the adjectives remain in the same case dictated by пожелать + indefinite object.
segments.p1-8-g182: Даже глупейшего intensifies the previous adjective: даже ('even') plus the comparative/superlative form глупейшего ('the more foolish / the most foolish' in context) continues the enumerated objects; case remains genitive as with пожелать себя + genitive-object.
segments.p1-8-g183: А именно is a common phrase meaning 'namely' or 'that is to say' and introduces a specification or clarification of the just-stated point.
segments.p1-8-g184: A content clause introduced by чтоб (colloquial variant of чтобы) expressing the specification: 'to have the right (иметь право) to wish for oneself even the most foolish (пожелать себе даже и глупейшего) and not be bound (не быть связанным) by the obligation (обязанностью — instrumental, showing 'by what one is bound') to desire for oneself only the sensible (желать себе одного только умного). Note the government: иметь право + infinitive; быть связанным + instrumental expresses being bound by something; один только умного is a substantive use of 'one' + adjective in genitive to mean 'only the sensible thing.'
segments.p1-8-g185: Ведь это глупейшее: ведь is a discourse particle 'after all/you see'; это is the demonstrative 'this/it'; глупейшее is the neuter superlative form used substantively 'the most foolish thing.' The phrase presents the referred phenomenon as concrete.
segments.p1-8-g186: Ведь это свой каприз repeats the particle + demonstrative and then свой (reflexive possessive) + noun каприз 'whim.' Свой refers back to the person previously discussed (i.e., it is his own whim); the phrase apposes/explains the preceding clause.
segments.p1-8-g187: И в самом деле is an idiomatic emphatic phrase meaning 'and indeed' or 'and truly'; в + noun самом деле (prepositional/locative idiom) forms the set expression 'really/indeed.'
segments.p1-8-g188: Господа here is again a vocative address 'gentlemen.' It is set off by commas and directly addresses the listeners; it does not alter the clause's syntax.
segments.p1-8-g189: Может быть (modal phrase 'may be') introduces a possibility; всего выгоднее is a comparative/superlative-like construction meaning 'most advantageous of all' (всего here functions as 'of all' / 'overall' modifying выгоднее). Для нашего брата uses для + genitive to mean 'for our brother/fellow.' Из всего repeats the 'of all' idea: 'of everything' (из + genitive). Together this yields 'may be the most advantageous of all for our brother of everything ...'.
segments.p1-8-g190: Что есть на земле is a relative clause 'that exists on the earth' modifying всего: что is the relative pronoun 'that', есть is the existential/presence verb 'is/exists', and на земле 'on earth' is a locative prepositional phrase. The clause restricts 'всего' to things on earth.
segments.p1-8-g191: Особенно в иных случаях is an adverbial phrase 'especially in other/certain cases.' Особенно (especially) modifies the whole preceding claim; в + plural cases (иных случаях, prepositional plural) gives the context/time in which the statement holds.
segments.p1-8-g143: Ведь is a discourse particle meaning “after all / you see,” used to justify or emphasize what follows; я is the first-person subject pronoun. The comma shows a pause before the parenthetical example.
segments.p1-8-g144: например is a parenthetical adverb meaning “for example,” marking that the speaker is about to give an instance.
segments.p1-8-g145: совершенно естественно is an adverbial phrase (“quite/utterly naturally”) modifying the verb хочу; хочу (present tense, 1st person singular) + infinitive жить expresses desire (“I want to live”). для того begins the purpose construction that will be completed by a clause with чтоб.
segments.p1-8-g146: чтоб introduces a purpose clause (“in order that / to”) followed by the perfective infinitive удовлетворить meaning “to satisfy/fulfil.” всей моей способности жить treats the capacity as a whole (всей … способности is a partitive/genitive phrasing meaning “the whole of my capacity to live”).
segments.p1-8-g147: а не для того sets up the contrastive negative purpose: “and not for the purpose of…,” with не negating the following intention.
segments.p1-8-g148: чтоб again introduces the alternative purpose. одной только моей рассудочной способности limits that purpose: только = “only,” рассудочной = “rational” (adjective), so the phrase means “only my rational capacity” (one single rational faculty rather than the whole capacity).
segments.p1-8-g149: то есть is a glossing phrase “that is / i.e.”; какой-нибудь + одной двадцатой доли is an indefinite fractional expression meaning “some one-twentieth part” (какой-нибудь softens it to “some/any”); всей моей способности жить repeats the whole-capacity phrase in genitive to show the fraction relates to the entire capacity to live.
segments.p1-8-g150: Что is the interrogative pronoun “what”; знает is present tense, 3rd person singular of знать (“to know”); рассудок is the subject “reason.” The word order (Что знает рассудок?) is a neutral Russian question (verb before subject).
segments.p1-8-g151: Рассудок знает только то is the main clause: Рассудок (subject) знает (present tense) and только то (“only that”) restricts the content of knowledge.
segments.p1-8-g152: что introduces a subordinate clause (“that which”). успел (past, perfective) + infinitive узнать means “has managed to learn/find out” (успел conveys completion/succeeded in doing it). иного is elliptical here meaning “anything else” (objective genitive).
segments.p1-8-g153: пожалуй is a parenthetical modal adverb meaning “perhaps” or “I suppose,” softening the statement.
segments.p1-8-g154: и никогда не узнает is a simple future-negation: и (“and/also”) + никогда не (never) + узнает (3rd person singular future) — “and will never learn.”
segments.p1-8-g155: это хоть и не утешение is a concessive phrase: это = “this/that,” хоть и = “even if / although,” and не утешение = “is not a consolation.” The construction admits the clause as possibly of no comfort.
segments.p1-8-g156: но отчего же этого и не высказать? is a rhetorical question: но = “but,” отчего = “why / for what reason,” же is an emphatic particle, этого (genitive) refers back to the proposition just stated, and и here adds a mild emphatic/colloquial ‘even/at least’ sense before the infinitive высказать (“to express/state”).
segments.p1-8-g157: This segment is just the comma continuation from the previous parenthesis; it functions as punctuation connecting the parenthetical remark to the following main clause.
segments.p1-8-g158: а introduces a contrast (“but”); натура человеческая places the noun before the adjective for emphasis: “human nature;” действует is present tense “acts/operates;” вся целиком is an intensifying phrase meaning “entirely/as a whole.”
segments.p1-8-g159: всем here is an instrumental/collective pronoun meaning “by/with everything” (i.e., using all that it has) and connects to the following relative clause.
segments.p1-8-g160: что в ней есть is a relative clause “that which is in it”: что = “that/which,” в = preposition “in,” ней = “it/her” (referring to nature), есть = existential verb “is/exists.”
segments.p1-8-g161: сознательно и бессознательно are adverbials “consciously and unconsciously,” coordinating with и and modifying действует.
segments.p1-8-g162: и хоть врет begins a concessive clause: и = “and,” хоть = “even if / though,” and врет (present 3rd sg) = “lies” (speaks untruths or deceives).
segments.p1-8-g163: да живет is a colloquial concessive-final remark: да here functions like an old-fashioned linking particle roughly “yet/still/and,” and живет (3rd sg) = “lives,” so the phrase means “and yet it lives” / “it nevertheless lives.”
segments.p1-8-g312: 'Рискнет' is 3rd person singular future ('will risk' / 'will venture'); the subject is understood from context ('he'). 'даже' is an emphatic adverb 'even'. 'пряниками' is instrumental plural and here indicates the motive or the small reward ('for gingerbreads' / 'for trivial treats') rather than a direct object. The conjunction 'и' links this clause to the following one; 'нарочно' (deliberately) modifies the verb 'пожелает'. 'пожелает' is also 3rd person singular future ('will wish'); the phrase 'самого пагубного вздора' is a genitive object after пожелать (пожелать чего) with 'самого' as an intensifier 'the very/most'.
segments.p1-8-g313: This is an appositional continuation of the object begun in the previous segment. 'самой' is the feminine/genitive form of 'самый' used as an intensifier 'the very'. 'неэкономической' is a genitive feminine adjective agreeing with 'бессмыслицы' and modifies its quality ('uneconomic'). 'бессмыслицы' is genitive singular ('of nonsense') completing the object phrase after пожелает.
segments.p1-8-g314: 'единственно' is an adverb meaning 'only/solely' and here restricts the purpose of the action. 'для того' begins a purposive expression 'for the purpose of...' and pairs with the following 'чтобы' clause; grammatically it's the standard 'для того, чтобы' pattern introducing intention.
segments.p1-8-g315: 'чтобы' introduces a subordinate clause of purpose ('in order to / so as to'). 'ко всему этому' uses the preposition 'ко' (allative variant of 'к') with the dative to mean 'to/into all this' — it points to the target into which something will be mixed. 'положительному благоразумию' is dative singular ('positive prudence' / 'that positive soundness of mind'), the noun 'благоразумие' qualified by 'положительному.' 'примешать' is the infinitive perfective 'to mix in / to add into' and takes the accusative object 'свой пагубный фантастический элемент' — 'свой' = 'his own' (refers back to the subject), 'пагубный' and 'фантастический' are attributive adjectives modifying 'элемент' = 'element' (object). Word order places the dative target before the infinitive and the possessive + adjectives before the noun object.
segments.p1-8-g316: A fronted nominal phrase for emphasis: 'Именно' is an intensifier ('precisely/exactly') modifying the following possessive noun phrase. 'свои' is a reflexive possessive agreeing with the implied subject ('he'), and 'фантастические мечты' is the noun head in the accusative/nominal list sense ('fantastic dreams'). The whole phrase highlights those dreams as what follows applies to.
segments.p1-8-g317: 'свою пошлейшую глупость' is another emphasized object (superlative adjective + noun). The finite verb 'пожелает' (perfective future) + infinitive 'удержать' forms a syntactic unit meaning 'will wish to keep.' 'удержать за собой' is an idiom meaning 'to keep to oneself.' 'единственно для того' ('only for the purpose') introduces the purposive relation that is completed by the following 'чтоб' clause.
segments.p1-8-g318: 'чтоб' is a subordinating conjunction expressing purpose ('in order that'). 'самому себе' is a dative reflexive with emphatic 'самому' (to himself personally). 'подтвердить' is an infinitive 'to confirm.' The parenthetical 'точно это так уж очень необходимо' is a colloquial aside: 'точно' here = 'as if,' and the string 'так уж очень необходимо' is an intensifying comment ('as if it were so very necessary').
segments.p1-8-g319: 'что' introduces the content (that-) clause being confirmed. The clause 'люди все еще люди' is a tautological copular assertion: 'люди' (people) + 'все' ('all/everyone') + 'еще' ('still') + repeating 'люди' to stress that they remain human. The word order intensifies the claim (subject + adverbials + predicative noun).
segments.p1-8-g320: 'а не' marks contrast ('and not'). The noun phrase 'фортепьянные клавиши' is the metaphorical predicate in nominative plural ('piano keys'), negating the alternative identity. Word order is compact: 'they are people, and not piano keys.'
segments.p1-8-g321: A relative clause modifying 'клавиши': 'на которых' = 'on which.' 'хоть и' is a concessive particle pair meaning 'even though' or 'although,' and 'играют' is present plural 'play.' 'сами законы природы собственноручно' is a heavily emphatic subject phrase: 'the laws of nature themselves, with their own hands' (sami + adverb собственноручно intensify that it is done personally/by the laws themselves).
segments.p1-8-g322: 'но' introduces the adversative/result clause ('but'). 'грозят' (present plural) here takes as subject the previously mentioned laws; it combines with the infinitive verb 'доиграться' (prefixed perfective) in an idiomatic sense: 'to play so much as to get into trouble.' 'до того' ('to that degree') intensifies the extent (literal 'up to that').
segments.p1-8-g323: 'что' introduces the result clause. 'уж' is an intensifier here ('indeed/actually'). 'мимо календаря' is an adverbial phrase ('past/beyond the calendar'), idiomatically meaning beyond ordinary measures; 'и' here functions as 'even/also' adding emphasis to the infinitive 'захотеть.' The impersonal negation 'ничего нельзя будет' uses 'нельзя' with future 'будет' plus the infinitive 'захотеть' to mean 'it will be impossible to want anything.' Word order places the adverbial ('мимо календаря') before the impersonal predicate for emphasis.
segments.p1-8-g324: A short exclamatory opening: Да here is a particle adding emphasis (not a literal 'yes'), ведь is a reinforcing particle 'after all', and мало того is a set phrase meaning 'that's not all' or 'moreover'; the colon marks that what follows expands or intensifies this point.
segments.p1-8-g325: даже ('even') introduces a concession; в том случае is the fixed prepositional phrase 'in that case' (в + том (demonstrative) + случае (prepositional) = 'in the case'). This frames a hypothetical scenario described next.
segments.p1-8-g326: A conditional clause: если ('if') introduces the hypothesis; он is the subject 'he'; действительно ('actually') intensifies the claim; бы is the conditional particle attached to the past-form verb to mark a counterfactual/hypothetical; оказался is the past tense perfective of оказаться 'to turn out/become', and фортепьянной клавишей is in the instrumental case (оказаться + instrumental) meaning 'to be/turn out to be a piano key'.
segments.p1-8-g327: Another conditional phrasing: если б (a colloquial/contracted form of если бы) introduces a subjunctive/hypothetical clause; это ('this') is the thing to be proven; доказать is the infinitive 'to prove' (here used impersonally: 'if one were to prove this'); ему is the dative 'to him'; даже естественными науками и математически is an adverbial phrase indicating means/instrumental proof ('even by the natural sciences and mathematically'), with естественными науками in the instrumental plural.
segments.p1-8-g328: так и ('even then' / 'still') + тут ('here' meaning 'in this matter') set up the result; не образумится is the negative future/perfective form of образумиться 'to come to one's senses' — in the hypothetical construction it means 'he still wouldn't be made sensible' or 'would not come to his senses'.
segments.p1-8-g329: а introduces contrast ('but'); нарочно напротив is a doubled adverbial emphasis meaning 'deliberately, on the contrary' (нарочно 'deliberately', напротив 'contrarily/on the contrary'); что-нибудь is the indefinite pronoun 'something' (что +-нибудь); сделает is future tense of сделать 'will do' — together 'but will deliberately do something opposite'.
segments.p1-8-g330: единственно 'only/solely' modifies the motive; из + одной неблагодарности means 'out of one (single) ingratitude' — из governs the genitive неблагодарности, with одной specifying 'one' (feminine genitive). The phrase gives the motive for the contrary action.
segments.p1-8-g331: собственно is a comment-like particle meaning 'actually' or 'in fact'; чтоб (colloquial short form of чтобы) introduces purpose: настоять (perfective infinitive) 'to insist (on)' + на своем is the idiomatic prepositional phrase meaning 'on his own [opinion/position]' (свой in the prepositional). The whole clause expresses that his deliberate act is done simply to be able to insist on his own view.
segments.p1-8-g332: А is a short connective particle here continuing the thought from the previous sentence (more like 'and'/'and yet'). В том случае is a fixed prepositional phrase meaning 'in that case' (в + demonstrative том + locative-case noun случай), setting the conditional frame for what follows.
segments.p1-8-g333: Если begins the conditional 'if'. Средств is genitive plural (partitive sense: 'means/resources') and with у него expresses possession ('he has [no] means'); the verb окажется is reflexive and here in the future tense meaning 'will turn out / be available'—so 'если средств у него не окажется' = 'if he doesn't have the means'. Note the negative particle не attaches to the verb.
segments.p1-8-g334: Выдумает is the perfective future (3rd person singular) of выдумать 'to invent/contrive', indicating a single completed action he will perform. Разрушение и хаос are its direct objects (inanimate nouns whose accusative forms match the nominative), listing what he will invent. The leading dash marks a slight pause or turn to consequences.
segments.p1-8-g335: The verb выдумает is repeated in parallel to the previous clause ('will invent ...'), with разные страдания as another accusative object meaning 'various sufferings' (разные here = 'various'). Настоит is the perfective future of настоять 'to insist (on)', and the particle таки after the verb adds colloquial emphasis ('still/after all/indeed'). На своём is an idiomatic phrase meaning 'on his own [point/opinion]', where свой in the locative refers back to the subject.
segments.p1-8-g383: 'Такая' (feminine form of 'такой') is fronted and, together with the enclitic particle 'ли', forms a rhetorical question/exclamation meaning 'is there such.../can ... be like that'. 'Своя воля' is a noun phrase where 'своя' is the reflexive possessive agreeing in gender/number/case with 'воля' and means 'one's own (will)'. 'Бывает' is the 3rd-person singular present of 'бывать' used here to mean 'to occur / to be (possible)', functioning as the predicate that asserts existence/possibility; there is no copula in present-tense Russian, so 'бывает' carries the predication. The overall structure thus asks, with emphasis, whether such a thing as one's own will ever occurs or can be like that.
