segments.p1-8-g254: Single-word, elliptical question using the adverb/short predicate 'пестро' meaning 'variegated' or 'motley'. It stands alone as a one-word interrogative (implied: 'Is it variegated?').
segments.p1-8-g255: 'Пожалуй' is an introductory modal particle meaning 'perhaps' or 'it may be', marking a tentative statement that links to the rest of the sentence.
segments.p1-8-g256: 'хоть и' is a concessive construction ('although/even if') and 'пестро' is used as a predicate/adverb meaning 'variegated' or 'motley'; together they mean 'even if (it) is variegated' (softly conceding the point).
segments.p1-8-g257: Infinitive 'разобрать' is used elliptically to mean 'to examine / if one examines'; 'только' here = 'just/merely'; 'во все века' = 'throughout all ages' (preposition 'во' used for time); 'и у всех народов' = 'and among all peoples' (prepositional phrase with 'у'); 'одни' emphasizes exclusivity ('nothing but'); 'парадные мундиры' = 'ceremonial uniforms'; 'на военных и статских' = 'on military and civil [people]' (the preposition 'на' with people indicates who wears them). The final parenthetical dash phrase 'уж одно это чего стоит' is idiomatic: 'this alone is something/worth noting' ('чего' is an exclamatory/genitive element and 'стоит' = 'is worth').
segments.p1-8-g258: 'А' = 'but' introduces the next, stronger point; 'с вицмундирами' = 'with the vic-mundirs' ('вицмундир' is a specialized/archaic term for a kind of ceremonial coat); 'и совсем' (with 'и' as intensifier + 'совсем' = 'altogether/completely'); the impersonal 'можно' = 'one can / it is possible' followed by infinitive 'сломать' with direct object 'ногу' — literal 'to break a leg' used hyperbolically: 'you could be completely undone/confused.'
segments.p1-8-g259: 'Ни один' is an emphatic negative ('not a single'); with the negation 'не' it yields 'not a single historian will withstand' — 'устоит' is the perfective-present form (here future sense: 'will be able to withstand'). The whole is an emphatic impersonal judgment: no historian could stand up to this.
segments.p1-8-g260: Another single-word, elliptical question; 'однообразно' is the neuter/adverbial form meaning 'monotonous' or 'uniform', used here as a predicate question ('Is it monotonous?').
segments.p1-8-g261: 'Ну' is a discourse marker/interjection ('well') used to preface the speaker's response or turn to a conclusion.
segments.p1-8-g262: 'Пожалуй' again is a tentative modal particle meaning 'perhaps' or 'I suppose', softening the statement that follows.
segments.p1-8-g263: 'И' here functions as a mild emphatic particle ('indeed/also'), and 'однообразно' is the predicate 'monotonous' — together forming 'and/indeed monotonous:' introducing the explanatory clause that follows.
segments.p1-8-g264: Present-tense reflexive verb 'дерутся' (3rd person plural) = 'they fight'; the repetition with 'да' (archaic/conjunctive 'and') 'дерутся да дерутся' is a colloquial rhetorical device meaning 'they fight and fight' (continuous/repeated action).
segments.p1-8-g265: 'И' = 'and'; 'теперь' = 'now'; 'дерутся' again = 'they are fighting' — it links temporal sequence: 'and now they fight.'
segments.p1-8-g266: 'И' = 'and'; 'прежде' = 'before/in the past'; 'дрались' is the past imperfective plural 'they fought' — marking that the fighting occurred previously as well.
segments.p1-8-g267: 'И' = 'and'; 'после' = 'afterwards/later'; 'дрались' again in the past — continuing the temporal list (they fought later, too).
segments.p1-8-g268: 'Согласитесь' is a 2nd-person plural/formal imperative or future-of-politeness form meaning 'agree (won't you?)' — a parenthetical appeal to the reader to accept the speaker's conclusion.
segments.p1-8-g269: Subordinate clause introduced by 'что' ('that'); 'это' = 'this/it'; stacked intensifiers 'даже уж слишком' = 'even/indeed too/far too'; 'однообразно' = 'monotonous' — overall: 'that this is even far too monotonous.'
segments.p1-8-g270: Одним словом is an idiomatic introductory phrase formed with the noun слово in the instrumental (одним словом) and functions adverbially: 'in a word / in short'. The instrumental case here is not literal but formulaic.
segments.p1-8-g271: все можно сказать о всемирной истории is an impersonal modal construction: можно + infinitive (сказать) expresses general possibility ('one can say / it is possible to say'). Все is the direct object meaning 'everything' and о всемирной истории is the prepositional phrase 'about world history'. Word order places the generalizing все at the front for emphasis.
segments.p1-8-g272: The repeated все is a resumptive/emphatic pronoun ('all, everything') pointing forward to the relative clause that follows; it's used to stress that every conceivable thing is meant.
segments.p1-8-g273: This is a relative/content clause introduced by что ('that/which' = 'whatever'). Только here is an intensifier meaning 'anything at all'/'whatever'; самому + дative (воображению) is an emphatic dative ('to the very imagination'), расстроенному is an attributive adjective agreeing with воображению ('disturbed/upset'); в голову is a phrase meaning 'into the head/mind'; может прийти is a modal + infinitive meaning 'may/ might come (to mind)'.
segments.p1-8-g274: Одного только is a genitive phrase meaning 'only one (thing)' — Одного is in the genitive because the impersonal нельзя (it is not possible) often takes a genitive for the thing excluded; нельзя сказать is the impersonal negative 'one cannot say' or 'it cannot be said'.
segments.p1-8-g275: Что introduces the content of what cannot be said: 'that ...'. Благоразумно is a short-form/neuter adjective/adverb used predicatively ('it is prudent' / 'prudently') with the copula omitted (a common Russian stylistic construction).
segments.p1-8-g276: На первом слове is a prepositional phrase literally 'on the first word' meaning 'at the very first word/as soon as the first word is heard'. Поперхнетесь is the future-form reflexive perfective verb (2nd-person plural or polite generic) of поперхнуться meaning 'to choke/cough (with surprise or literal choking)'; together it expresses 'you will choke at the first word.'
segments.p1-8-g277: И даже вот какая тут штука поминутно встречается is a colloquial opening: И даже вот какая тут штука = 'and here's even what sort of thing' (вот + какая is a demonstrative exclamatory frame). Тут is 'here/at this point', штука is a colloquial 'thing/matter', поминутно встречается = 'occurs every minute' or 'keeps cropping up'. The verb встречается is reflexive/passive-like meaning 'is encountered/appears'.
segments.p1-8-g278: постоянно ведь являются в жизни такие благонравные и благоразумные люди: постоянно ('constantly') and ведь ('after all/you see') add emphasis. Являются в жизни = 'appear in life' (являются used like 'turn up' or 'are found in life'). Такие + adjectives + люди = 'such ... people.'
segments.p1-8-g279: Такие мудрецы и любители рода человеческого lists examples: 'such sages and lovers of the human race.' Род человеческого is a genitive phrase meaning 'of the human kind/race.' The adjectives and nouns agree in number and case.
segments.p1-8-g280: которые именно задают себе целью всю жизнь вести себя как можно благонравнее и благоразумнее is a relative clause describing those people. Которые = 'who'; именно gives precision/emphasis ('precisely'); задают себе целью = 'set as their goal' (задавать цель кому-либо = to set a goal for oneself); всю жизнь вести себя = 'to spend their whole life behaving' (вести себя = 'to behave'); как можно + comparative = 'as ... as possible.'
segments.p1-8-g281: так сказать is a parenthetical idiom meaning 'so to speak' or 'as it were'; it's composed of так ('so') + infinitive сказать used idiomatically.
segments.p1-8-g282: светить собой ближним is a figurative infinitive phrase: светить ('to shine') with собой ('by/with themselves') and ближним ('to their neighbors/fellow humans') — 'to illuminate/shine by their own example for others.' Светить собой is idiomatic for setting oneself up as an example.
segments.p1-8-g283: собственно для того is a linking phrase: собственно here means 'properly/precisely/strictly speaking' and для того ('for that') introduces the purpose clause that follows (i.e., 'for the very purpose of ...').
segments.p1-8-g284: чтоб доказать им is a purpose clause introduced by чтоб (colloquial contraction of чтобы = 'in order that / to') plus the infinitive доказать ('to prove') and the dative pronoun им ('to them').
segments.p1-8-g285: что действительно можно на свете прожить и благонравно gives the content of what they want to prove. Что = 'that'; действительно = 'really/indeed'; можно + infinitive прожить = 'it is possible to live'; на свете = 'in the world'; and благонравно is an adverbial/short adjective meaning 'virtuously' — the clause states the claim 'that one can in fact live in the world virtuously.'
segments.p1-8-g286: и благоразумно closes the sentence by adding the parallel adverbial predicate 'and prudently.' The short/neuter form благоразумно is used predicatively in parallel to благонравно.
segments.p1-8-g192: 'А' is a discourse conjunction here best rendered 'and' (it links back to the previous thought). 'В частности' is a fixed phrase meaning 'in particular' (в + noun in genitive — 'particularity'), placed at the clause beginning for focus.
segments.p1-8-g193: The modal phrase 'может быть' = 'may be' (possibility). 'Выгоднее' is the comparative of 'выгодный' ('more advantageous'). 'всех выгод' uses 'всех' + genitive plural to mean 'of all advantages' (archaic plural 'выгод'). 'даже и' intensifies ('even'), and 'в таком случае' = 'in such a case' (в + locative/prepositional 'таком случае').
segments.p1-8-g194: 'Если' introduces a conditional clause ('if'). Two present-tense verbs govern the clause: 'приносит' (3rd sg. indicative — 'brings/causes') with indirect object 'нам' ('to us'), and 'противоречит' (3rd sg. — 'contradicts'). 'Противоречит' takes the dative 'самым здравым заключениям' ('the very soundest conclusions'); 'о выгодах' is a prepositional phrase 'about advantages'.
segments.p1-8-g195: 'Потому что' is the causal conjunction 'because'. 'Во всяком случае' is an idiom meaning 'in any case/at any rate' (vo- variant of в for euphony). 'Сохраняет' is 3rd sg. present 'preserves/saves' with dative 'нам' ('for us'); 'самое главное и самое дорогое' are neuter nominalized adjectives functioning as direct objects: 'the most important and the dearest.'
segments.p1-8-g196: 'То есть' is the explanatory phrase 'that is' (lit. 'that is'). Here 'есть' is the present of 'быть' used in the idiom; the following nouns 'нашу личность и нашу индивидуальность' are accusative objects with the possessive 'нашу' ('our'), specifying what is preserved.
segments.p1-8-g197: 'Иные' is a plural nominative meaning 'some others/others (people)'. 'Вот' is a discourse particle adding immediacy or pointing ('you see'/'well'). 'Утверждают' is 3rd pl. present 'assert/claim'.
segments.p1-8-g198: 'Что' introduces reported speech ('that'). 'Это' = 'this/it'. The phrase 'и в самом деле' = 'indeed/in truth' (и adds emphasis). 'Всего дороже' functions as 'dearer than anything else' (всего intensifies the comparative). 'Для человека' = 'for a person'.
segments.p1-8-g199: 'Хотенье' is an older nominal form of 'хотение' (desire/wish) in the nominative; it is the subject/topic that the sentence is about.
segments.p1-8-g200: 'Конечно' is a parenthetical adverb meaning 'of course' or 'naturally', qualifying the following possibility.
segments.p1-8-g201: 'Может' here functions as a modal 'may/can' (short for 'может быть' in this construction), indicating possibility/capacity.
segments.p1-8-g202: 'Если хочет' is a conditional subordinate ('if it/one wants'); 'хочет' is 3rd sg. present and grammatically refers back to 'хотенье' (treated as the agent).
segments.p1-8-g203: 'И сходиться с рассудком' — 'и' links the verb, 'сходиться' is the infinitive meaning 'to agree/come into accord', and it takes 'с' + instrumental 'рассудком' ('with reason').
segments.p1-8-g204: 'Особенно' ('especially') introduces a condition: 'если не злоупотреблять этим' is an infinitival negative condition ('if one does not abuse it'); 'злоупотреблять' governs instrumental 'этим' ('this/it').
segments.p1-8-g205: 'А' contrasts with the previous warning ('but'), 'пользоваться' is an infinitive 'to use/avail oneself of' and here implies using 'it' (referencing 'этим'); 'умеренно' is an adverb 'moderately'.
segments.p1-8-g206: 'Это' ('this') is the demonstrative subject: 'и полезно и даже иногда похвально' uses the correlative 'и ... и' ('both ... and') with predicates 'полезно' ('useful') and 'похвально' ('praiseworthy'), 'даже иногда' adds the nuance 'even sometimes'.
segments.p1-8-g207: 'Но' is a contrasting conjunction ('but'). 'Хотенье' (nominative) is the subject 'desire'. The adverbial cluster 'очень часто и даже большею частию' means 'very often, and even for the most part' (большею частию is an archaic instrumental/genitive expression meaning 'mostly'). 'Совершенно и упрямо' are adverbial modifiers ('completely and stubbornly') modifying the verb 'разногласит' (3rd sg.), which takes 'с' + instrumental 'рассудком' ('is at odds with reason'). The final conjunction 'и' is left hanging for stylistic trailing-off.
segments.p1-8-g210: A single conjunction 'и' ('and') standing alone as a stylistic fragment; it leaves the thought unfinished and functions as a trailing connector.
segments.p1-8-g365: А is a connective particle here meaning "and/then" leading into the remark; после этого is a temporal prepositional phrase with этого in the genitive after после ("after this/that"). Как introduces a rhetorical question meaning "how (can one)..." and is followed by the negative infinitive не согрешить (perfective infinitive), which here expresses a rhetorical: "how can one not sin / how could it be not a sin."
segments.p1-8-g366: Не похвалить is another negative infinitive forming a parallel rhetorical clause with the previous one: "how can one not praise..." The subject is omitted (elliptical), so the infinitive carries the evaluative force (it would be wrong/not possible not to praise).
segments.p1-8-g367: Что introduces a reported-content clause: "that..." Этого (genitive) goes with existential нет to mean "this is not (there) yet" (нет + genitive = "there is no/there is not"). The conjunction и links a second clause introduced by что. Хотенье is the older spelling of хотение (nominative) "desire/wish"; покамест is an archaised adverb meaning "for the time being." Черт знает от чего is a colloquial parenthetical "the devil knows from what" (= "who knows what") and depends syntactically as the object of зависит; зависит is 3rd person singular present of зависеть "to depend (on)."
segments.p1-8-g370: Вы кричите мне is a straightforward 2nd-person plural/polite present: "you shout at me" (кричите is present indicative). The parenthetical starts with если ("if") and uses the perfective future удостоите (2nd plural) of удостоить plus instrumental вашим криком to mean "if you will still deign/grant me your shout" — a sarcastic concessive conditional in parentheses.
segments.p1-8-g371: Что here introduces reported content: "that..." Ведь is a particle adding emphasis/"after all." Тут "here/at this point." Никто ... не снимает is a negative construction meaning "no one removes/takes away"; с + genitive (с меня) means "from me," so с меня воли не снимает = "nobody takes my will away" (i.e. nobody relieves me of my will/freedom).
segments.p1-8-g372: Что тут только и хлопочут is an idiomatic construction: тут только и X means "here all they do is X" — хлопочут (present) "they fuss/are busy (about)." Как-нибудь так is a colloquial adverbial phrase "somehow like that / in some such way," with как-нибудь = "somehow" and так adding vague manner. Устроить is the infinitive "to arrange/engineer" — they bustle about to manage things somehow.
segments.p1-8-g373: Чтоб introduces a purpose or result clause (often rendered in English as a subjunctive): "so that / in order that." Воля моя сама is the subject phrase: воля (feminine) with the possessive моя; сама is emphatic/reflexive here meaning "itself" ("my will itself").
segments.p1-8-g374: Своей собственной волей is an instrumental noun phrase: своей (reflexive possessive referring back to the subject) and собственной (intensifying "own/proper") modify волей (instrumental of воля). The instrumental here marks means/manner: "by/with its own will."
segments.p1-8-g375: Совпадала is the past form agreeing with the feminine воля, used after чтоб to express desired/resultant matching (often translated with English subjunctive/would): "would coincide." С + instrumental (с моими нормальными интересами) means "with my normal interests," with моими and нормальными in the instrumental plural agreeing with интересами.
segments.p1-8-g376: С законами природы и с арифметикой continues the list with repeated с + instrumental meaning "with the laws of nature and with arithmetic." Законами природы is instrumental plural + genitive "of nature;" арифметикой is instrumental singular meaning "arithmetic."
segments.p1-8-g377: Эх is an interjection expressing a sigh or exclamation; here it opens the remark and signals a slightly ironic or resigned tone. It stands alone and does not affect the sentence grammar beyond discourse marking.
segments.p1-8-g378: господа is the vocative plural 'gentlemen' addressing the listeners; Russian uses the nominative form here for direct address. It's a discourse marker that frames the following rhetorical question.
segments.p1-8-g379: какая уж тут своя воля будет is a rhetorical question: какая ... будет = 'what sort/kind ... will there be'. Уж is a modal particle adding emphasis or resignation ('after all/indeed'), тут is locative 'here' or 'in this situation', своя воля = 'one's own will', and будет is the future copula 'will be'. The whole clause asks, in effect, 'what sort of one's own will can exist here?'.
segments.p1-8-g380: когда дело доходит до таблички и до арифметики is a temporal/concessive clause introduced by когда 'when' and using the idiom дело доходит до ... = 'when it comes to ...'. Дело here means 'the matter/point', доходит (present tense) = 'reaches' or 'comes up', and the repeated до before each noun gives parallelism: 'up to the little table and up to arithmetic.'
segments.p1-8-g381: когда будет одно только дважды два четыре в ходу? is a second subordinate clause introduced by когда. Будет is the future copula 'there will be'; одно только 'just one thing' (a single thing only); дважды два четыре is an elliptic citation of the arithmetic statement 'two times two equals four', treated as a noun-phrase; в ходу is an idiom 'in use/in operation/in vogue'. The clause asks 'when there will be nothing but "2×2=4" operating.'
segments.p1-8-g382: This short sentence places the arithmetic phrase (Дважды два) first as the subject/topic, followed by the parenthetical/added phrase и без моей воли 'even without my will' (и here functioning as 'even/also'), and ends with the future copula будет. The preposition без governs the genitive моей воли. The overall structure asserts that the arithmetic truth holds regardless of the speaker's will.
segments.p1-8-g336: "Проклятие" is a nominative noun meaning 'a curse'; "пустит" is the perfective future 3rd-person singular of "пустить" meaning 'will send/let out'; together with the adverbial phrase "по свету" it forms the idiom 'to let loose upon the world' (subject → verb → adverbial).
segments.p1-8-g337: The conjunction "а так как" = 'and/since' introduces a causal clause. "проклинать" is the infinitive 'to curse' used with modal "может" ('can/ is able to'), "только один человек" = 'only one person'. The parenthesis begins with the demonstrative "это" + emphatic particle "уж" ('indeed'), followed by possessive "его" and noun "привилегия" ('privilege').
segments.p1-8-g338: "главнейшим образом" is an adverbial phrase meaning 'chiefly/primarily'; "отличающая" is a present active participle (feminine nominative) modifying "привилегия" and means 'distinguishing'; "его" here is the object 'him'; "от других животных" = 'from other animals' (preposition + plural).
segments.p1-8-g339: "так ведь он," is a resumptive phrase returning to the main subject: "так"/'so' + discourse particle "ведь"/'after all' + subject "он"/'he', with a pause shown by the comma.
segments.p1-8-g340: "пожалуй" is a modal particle meaning 'perhaps' or 'it may be', marking the following assertion as tentative.
segments.p1-8-g341: "одним проклятием" is an instrumental-phrase meaning 'by a single curse' (instrumental = means); "достигнет" is future 3rd-person singular perfective 'will attain/achieve'; "своего" is a neuter pronoun here standing for 'what he wants / his end' (idiom: "достичь своего").
segments.p1-8-g342: "то есть" is the explanatory phrase 'that is'; "действительно" is an adverb 'truly/indeed'; "убедится" is the future-reflexive perfective 'will become convinced' (убедиться), giving the result of the preceding clause.
segments.p1-8-g343: Subordinate clause introduced by the conjunction "что" ('that') stating the content of his conviction: subject "он" + predicate-noun "человек" ('a man/person').
segments.p1-8-g344: Contrastive exclamation: "а" = 'and/but', negation "не" before the noun phrase "фортепьянная клавиша" ('piano key') — a metaphorical contrast to being a living man.
segments.p1-8-g345: "Если вы скажете," is the protasis 'If you say' with "Если" (if) and future/conditional "скажете" (you will say); "вы" is the polite/plural you. It introduces a hypothetical.
segments.p1-8-g346: "что и это все можно рассчитать по табличке" is a content clause: "что"('that'), "и это все" = 'even all this/this too', "можно" = impersonal 'it is possible/can', "рассчитать" infinitive 'to calculate/figure out' and "по табличке" = 'by/according to a little table' (instrumental/according-to usage of "по").
segments.p1-8-g347: "и хаос," simply lists 'chaos' as one of the items that could be 'calculated'; the conjunction "и" here means 'and/also'.
segments.p1-8-g348: "и мрак," continues the list with 'gloom/darkness'; again "и" = 'and'.
segments.p1-8-g349: "и проклятие," completes the list with 'and a curse' (the same noun used earlier).
segments.p1-8-g350: "так уж одна возможность предварительного расчета все остановит и рассудок возьмет свое" — "так уж" is a colloquial 'well/indeed then', "одна возможность предварительного расчета" = 'that one possibility of preliminary calculation'; "все остановит" = 'will stop everything'; "и рассудок возьмет свое" = idiomatically 'and reason will take its own / have its way' ("возьмет" future of "взять").
segments.p1-8-g351: The dash leads to the consequence: "так человек нарочно сумасшедшим на этот случай сделается," — "так"/'then', "человек"/'a man', "нарочно"/'deliberately', "сумасшедшим сделается" uses the adjectival instrumental and reflexive verb "сделается" ('will make himself / will become') to mean 'will make himself mad' for that specific case.
segments.p1-8-g352: "чтоб не иметь рассудка и настоять на своем!" is a purpose/result clause introduced by "чтоб" ('in order that/so that'): "не иметь рассудка" = 'not to have reason/sanity', and "настоять на своем" = 'to insist on one's own (opinion)'; both verbs are infinitives indicating intended outcome.
segments.p1-8-g353: "Я верю в это," is a simple declarative: subject "Я" + verb "верю" ('I believe') + prepositional object "в это" ('in this/that').
segments.p1-8-g354: "я отвечаю за это," literal 'I answer for it' = 'I vouch for it / I am responsible for that'; "отвечаю за" here means 'to vouch for / to stand behind'.
segments.p1-8-g355: "потому что ведь все дело-то человеческое," combines "потому что" ('because') with emphatic "ведь" ('after all'); "все дело-то человеческое" literally 'the whole matter is human' = 'the whole business is a human one' ("-то" is an emphasizing particle attached to "дело").
segments.p1-8-g356: "кажется," is a parenthetical verb 'it seems/appears' marking the speaker's tentative assertion.
segments.p1-8-g357: "и действительно в том только и состоит," is the main predicative phrase: "и действительно" = 'indeed/actually', "в том только и состоит" is an idiom 'consists only in this' (the repetition of "и" emphasizes exclusivity).
segments.p1-8-g358: "чтоб человек поминутно доказывал себе," uses "чтоб" to introduce the idea/purpose and pairs it with the past-imperfect form "доказывал" to express habitual/repeated action ('that a person should prove to himself'); "поминутно" = 'every minute', "себе" = dative reflexive 'to himself'.
segments.p1-8-g359: Clause "что он человек," restates the content to be proved: conjunction "что" ('that') + pronoun "он" + noun "человек" ('a man/person').
segments.p1-8-g360: "а не штифтик!" is the sharp contrastive exclamation 'and not a little pin!' (a diminutive metaphor implying a mere mechanical peg rather than a human being), with "а" marking contrast and "не" negation.
segments.p1-8-g361: "хоть своими боками," uses the concessive particle "хоть" ('even/at least'), and the instrumental plural "своими боками" ('with his own sides' / 'with his body/ flanks') to indicate a physical means or manner.
segments.p1-8-g362: "да доказывал;" here "да" functions colloquially as 'and/yet' linking to the previous concessive phrase, and "доказывал" is the imperfective past form expressing repeated or ongoing proving ('he would/kept proving').
segments.p1-8-g363: "хоть троглодитством," again uses "хоть" ('even/at least') plus the instrumental noun "троглодитством" ('by troglodyte behavior/savagery') to indicate the manner or means — even by brutal conduct.
segments.p1-8-g364: Repeat of the connective and verb: "да доказывал." — "да" links, and "доказывал" repeats the sense 'he proved/kept proving', closing the sentence with a resumptive habitual action.
